'''Ben Evans''' (born 30 October 1992), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"Bev"''', is a Welsh former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[North Wales Crusaders]] in the [[RFL League 1]] and [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at international level.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

He has previously played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[Super League|top flight]], at the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]]. Evans has also played for the [[London Broncos]] and [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].

==Background==
Evans was born in [[Bridgend]], Wales.

He is the twin brother of the  rugby league footballer; [[Rhys Evans (rugby league)|Rhys Evans]].

==Playing career==
===Warrington Wolves===
Evans signed for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] alongside his twin brother [[Rhys Evans (rugby league)|Rhys Evans]], and progressed through the youth system, appearing for the first-team in several pre-season friendlies but never in a competitive match.

'''2014'''
Evans featured in round 1 against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] to round 5 [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]. He then featured in round 7 against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] and then in round 9 [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] to round 17 Salford. Evans featured in round 4 [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] to the Quarter Final against [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[Challenge Cup]].

'''2015'''
He featured in round 2 [[Hull F.C.]] and then in round 4 Hull Kingston Rovers to round 6 against St Helens.

===Bradford Bulls===
In 2013 he was offered the chance to go to another [[Super League]] side on loan, [[Bradford Bulls]]. Ben signed a 1-year loan deal with Bradford before pre-season started in time for the 2013 season. However, his début was delayed as a hip injury ruled him out for the start of the season. 
[[File:Ben Evans.jpg|thumb|right|Evans playing for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in 2013]]
He left at the end of the season to return to his parent club Warrington where he has played this season repeatedly and scored 1 try.<ref name="Bradford Bulls prop Ben Evans to miss start of the season">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/20937217|title=Bradford Bulls prop Ben Evans to miss start of the season|publisher=bbc.co.uk|date=7 January 2013   |accessdate=1 January 2014}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
In 2016, Evans found himself at the bottom of the pecking order at Warrington not playing a Super League game all season. He played 3 games on loan to [[Leigh Centurions]], and also on loan for 2 games for [[Rochdale Hornets]].

On 16 October 2016, Evans signed for the [[London Broncos]] for the 2017 season.<ref name="London Broncos Rugby League : London sign Warrington’s Ben Evans">{{cite web|url=http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/london-sign-warringtons-ben-evans|title=London Broncos Rugby League : London sign Warrington's Ben Evans|publisher=londonbroncosrl.com|date=7 October 2016|accessdate=1 January 2017}}</ref>

===North Wales Crusaders===
On 8 Dec 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[North Wales Crusaders]] in the [[RFL League 1]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=8 Dec 2023|title=League 1 move confirmed for veteran Wales international with appearances at last three World Cups|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/league-1-move-confirmed-for-veteran-wales-international-with-appearances-at-last-three-world-cups}}</ref>

==International career==
Having represented [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at youth level, in 2012 Ben was called up by the senior squad to play in the [[2012 Autumn International Series]]. He made his international début in a 20-6 defeat by [[France national rugby league team|France]], and made a further appearance against [[England national rugby league team|England]] that year. He made his international début before making a first-team appearance at club level.

He was selected in Wales [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] squad. He featured in the 32-16 defeat by Italy and in the 16-24 defeat by the United States. He scored against Italy (1 try).

He was selected in the [[Wales 9s]] squad for the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].<ref>{{cite web|date=9 October 2019|title=16-man squad for 9s World Cup revealed|url=https://www.walesrugbyleague.co.uk/article/16722/-man-squad-for-s-world-cup-revealed|publisher=Wales RL|accessdate=31 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191010051335/https://www.walesrugbyleague.co.uk/article/16722/-man-squad-for-s-world-cup-revealed|archive-date=10 October 2019|url-status=dead}}</ref>
